q:

question:

(assuming ideality)
solution:
K P = K C RT Δ n g

SO 2 (g)+ 1 2 O 2 (g)⇌ SO 3 g

Δ n g =1−(1+ 1 2 )=- 1 2

subject: Chemistry
q:
$8 \mathrm{~mol}$ of $A B_3(\mathrm{~g})$ are introduced into a $1.0 \mathrm{dm}^3$ vessel. If it dissociates as
$2 \mathrm{AB}_3(g) \rightleftharpoons A_2(g)+3 B_2(g)$. At equilibrium, $2 \mathrm{~mol}$ of $A_2$ are found to be present. The equilibrium constant of this reaction is
question:
$8 \mathrm{~mol}$ of $A B_3(\mathrm{~g})$ are introduced into a $1.0 \mathrm{dm}^3$ vessel. If it dissociates as
$2 \mathrm{AB}_3(g) \rightleftharpoons A_2(g)+3 B_2(g)$. At equilibrium, $2 \mathrm{~mol}$ of $A_2$ are found to be present. The equilibrium constant of this reaction is
solution:
$2 A B_3(g) \leftrightharpoons A_2(g)+3 B_2(g)$
at $t=0 \quad 8 \quad 0 \quad 0$
at eq. $(8-2 \times 2) \quad 2 \quad 3 \times 2$ $=4 \quad 2 \quad 6$
now $K_C=\frac{\left[A_2\right]\left[B_2\right]^3}{\left[A B_3\right]^2}=\frac{2 / 1 \times[6 / 1]^3}{[4 / 1]^2}=27$

solution:
X(g) ⇌ Y(g) + Z(g)  Kp1=3

 n                                   Initial concentration

n-αn    αn    αn     at equilibrium
Let total pressure be p1

Using dalton's law of partial pressures and equation for equilibrium constant:
∴Kp1=α1+α×p121-α1+αp1

⇒3=α2×p11-α2       ....(X)

A(g)⇌2 B( g)  kp2=1

Let total pressure be p2
Using dalton's law of partial pressures and equation for equilibrium constant

⇒kp1kp2=p14p2

⇒31=p14p2

∴p1p2=12

subject: Chemistry
q:
For the following Assertion and Reason, the correct option is

Assertion (A) : When Cu (II) and sulphide ions are mixed, they react together extremely quickly to give a solid.

Reason (R) : The equilibrium constant of Cu2+aq+S2-aq⇌Cuss is high because the solubility product is low.
question:
For the following Assertion and Reason, the correct option is

Assertion (A) : When Cu (II) and sulphide ions are mixed, they react together extremely quickly to give a solid.

Reason (R) : The equilibrium constant of Cu2+aq+S2-aq⇌Cuss is high because the solubility product is low.
options: Both (A) and (R) are true but (R) is not the explanation for (A).
Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the explanation for (A).
solution: Rate of chemical reaction has nothing to do with value of equilibrium constant.

subject: Chemistry
q: In which of the following reactions, an increase in the volume of the container will favour the formation of products?
question: In which of the following reactions, an increase in the volume of the container will favour the formation of products?
solution:
Volume $\uparrow P \downarrow$, reaction proceeds in which direction where the number of moles of gases increases.
$2 \mathrm{NO}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NO}(\mathrm{g})+\mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g})$
$\Delta \mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{g}}=(2+1)-2=1$

$$
White precipitate of $\mathrm{AgCl}$ appears on adding which of the following?

$$

solution:

$$

$$

subject: Chemistry
q:
Consider the following gaseous equilibrium in a closed container of volume ' V ' at $\mathrm{T}(\mathrm{K})$.
$\mathrm{P}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{Q}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{PQ}(\mathrm{g})$
2 moles each of $\mathrm{P}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}), \mathrm{Q}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})$ and $\mathrm{PQ}(\mathrm{g})$ are present at equilibrium. Now one mole each of ' $\mathrm{P}_{2}$ ' and ' $\mathrm{Q}_{2}$ ' are added to the equilibrium keeping the temperature at $\mathrm{T}(\mathrm{K})$. The number of moles of $P_{2}, Q_{2}$ and $P Q$ at the new equilibrium, respectively, are
question:
Consider the following gaseous equilibrium in a closed container of volume ' V ' at $\mathrm{T}(\mathrm{K})$.
$\mathrm{P}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{Q}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{PQ}(\mathrm{g})$
2 moles each of $\mathrm{P}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}), \mathrm{Q}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})$ and $\mathrm{PQ}(\mathrm{g})$ are present at equilibrium. Now one mole each of ' $\mathrm{P}_{2}$ ' and ' $\mathrm{Q}_{2}$ ' are added to the equilibrium keeping the temperature at $\mathrm{T}(\mathrm{K})$. The number of moles of $P_{2}, Q_{2}$ and $P Q$ at the new equilibrium, respectively, are
solution:
Initial equilibrium: 2 moles each of $P_2$, $Q_2$, $PQ$ with $K = \frac{[PQ]^2}{[P_2][Q_2]} = \frac{4}{4} = 1$.
After adding 1 mole each of $P_2$ and $Q_2$: initial composition is $(3, 3, 2)$. Reaction quotient $Q = \frac{4}{9} < K$, so reaction shifts right.
Let x moles of reactants combine:
$\frac{(2+2x)^2}{(3-x)^2} = 1$
$2+2x = 3-x$
$3x = 1$ → $x = \frac{1}{3}$
Final moles: $P_2 = 3 - \frac{1}{3} = 2.67$, $Q_2 = 2.67$, $PQ = 2 + \frac{2}{3} = 2.67$

subject: Chemistry
q:
The equilibrium constant for the reaction
$$
\mathrm{SO}_3(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{SO}_2(\mathrm{~g})+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g})
$$
is $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{c}}=4.9 \times 10^{-2}$. The value of $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{c}}$ for the reaction
$$
2 \mathrm{SO}_2(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{SO}_3(\mathrm{~g})
$$ 
will be
question:
The equilibrium constant for the reaction
$$
\mathrm{SO}_3(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{SO}_2(\mathrm{~g})+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g})
$$
is $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{c}}=4.9 \times 10^{-2}$. The value of $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{c}}$ for the reaction
$$
2 \mathrm{SO}_2(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{SO}_3(\mathrm{~g})
$$ 
will be
solution: $\begin{aligned} \mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{c}}^{\prime} & =\left(\frac{1}{4.9 \times 10^{-2}}\right)^2 \\ & =\frac{10^4}{4.9 \times 4.9}=\frac{100 \times 100}{24.01} \\ & =4.1649 \times 100 \\ & =416.49\end{aligned}$

subject: Chemistry
q: What happens when an inert gas is added to an equilibrium keeping volume unchanged?
question: What happens when an inert gas is added to an equilibrium keeping volume unchanged?
solution: On adding inert gas at constant volume the total pressure of the system is increased, but the partial pressure of each reactant and product remains the same. Hence no effect on the state of equilibrium.

2Ss+3O2g⇌2SO3g; K2=10129

The equilibrium constant for the reaction, 2SO2g+O2g⇌2SO3g is:
question:

solution:
Ss+O2g⇌SO2g; k1=1052 ……(i)

keq=SO32SO22O2

keq=k2k12=1012910522=1025

subject: Chemistry
q:
The effect of addition of helium gas to the following reaction in equilibrium state at constant volume, is :
PCI5( g)⇌PCl3( g)+Cl2( g)
question:
The effect of addition of helium gas to the following reaction in equilibrium state at constant volume, is :
PCI5( g)⇌PCl3( g)+Cl2( g)
options: the equilibrium will shift in the forward direction and more of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>Cl</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>PCl</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> gases will be produced.
the equilibrium will go backward due to suppression of dissociation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>PCl</mi><mn>5</mn></msub></math>.
addition of helium will not affect the equilibrium.
solution:
PCI5( g)⇌PCl3( g)+Cl2( g)
In the above reaction vessel, when helium(an inert gas) is added following cases can happen for isobaric and isochoric processes respectively:

Case 1 : At constant P - volume will increase so reaction will shift in forward direction then answer will be A

Case 2 : At constant volume no change in active mass so reaction will not shift in any direction then answer will be D.

Thus, at constant volume, addition of helium will not have any impact on the equilibrium.

subject: Chemistry
q:
The number of correct statement/s involving equilibria in physical processes from the following is ________

(A) Equilibrium is possible only in a closed system at a given temperature.

(B) Both the opposing processes occur at the same rate.

(C) When equilibrium is attained at a given temperature, the value of all its parameters became equal

(D) For dissolution of solids in liquids, the solubility is constant at a given temperature.
question:
The number of correct statement/s involving equilibria in physical processes from the following is ________

(A) Equilibrium is possible only in a closed system at a given temperature.

(B) Both the opposing processes occur at the same rate.

(C) When equilibrium is attained at a given temperature, the value of all its parameters became equal

(D) For dissolution of solids in liquids, the solubility is constant at a given temperature.
solution:
The equilibrium is possible when there is no exchange of matter between system and surroundings.

At equilibrium state the forward and backward processes occur with same rate (speed)

When a system reaches equilibrium at a given temperature, the forward and reverse reactions are occurring at the same rate, and the concentrations (or partial pressures) of the reactants and products no longer change with time. At this point, the system has achieved a steady state, where the rate of the forward reaction is equal to the rate of the reverse reaction. So when equilibrium is attained, the value of all its parameters became constant.

The solubility is function of solubility product, the value of which is constant at a given temperature.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Water decomposes at 2300 K

H2Og→H2g+12O2g

The percent of water decomposing at 2300 K and 1 bar is _____ (Nearest integer). Equilibrium constant for the reaction is 2×10-3 at 2300 K
question:
Water decomposes at 2300 K

H2Og→H2g+12O2g

The percent of water decomposing at 2300 K and 1 bar is _____ (Nearest integer). Equilibrium constant for the reaction is 2×10-3 at 2300 K
solution:
Decomposition of water gives:
H2Og ⇌ H2g + 12O2g

P01-α    P0α       P0α2  partial pr. at eq.

Thus, P01+α2=1            …1

Kp=PH2PO21/2PH2O

P0αP0α21/2P01-α=2×10-3

since α is negligible w.r.t 1 thus, 1-α≈1

⇒αα2=2×10-3

⇒α3/2=23/2×10-3

⇒α=23/2×2/3×10-3×2/3

⇒α=2×10-2,      %α=2%

solution:
Cl2⇌2Cl

Let mol of both of Cl2 and Cl is x

PCl=x2x×1=12 atm

PCl2=x2x×1=12 atm

Kp=12212=12=0.5⇒5×10-1

Equilibrium constant for the reaction is ______.
question: The following concentrations were observed at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>500</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></math> for the formation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>NH</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> from <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>. At equilibrium : <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="]" open="["><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mo>,</mo><mfenced close="]" open="["><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="]" open="["><msub><mi>NH</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi></math>. Equilibrium constant for the reaction is ______.
solution:
The equilibrium constant of concentration (denoted by Kc) of a chemical reaction at equilibrium can be defined as the ratio of the concentration of products to the concentration of the reactants, each raised to their respective stoichiometric coefficients.

The equilibrium reaction is 

N2g+3H2g⇋2NH3g

Kc=NH32H23N2

  =32×10-223×10-23×2×10-2

   =32×10-422×33×10-6×2×10-2

 =14×2×3×104=1000024=416.6≈417
